business.headline: Standard deduction, NPS and more: 5 tax benefits still available under new regimeChoosing the new tax regime does not necessarily mean giving up every tax-saving opportunity. Although most popular deductions are no longer available, a few key benefits remain that can help lower your taxable income.

Finance Ministry holds borrowing plans steady, to reassess spending in OctoberSources said the Budget 2026-27 already factored in global uncertainties, giving the government room to handle external shocks.

Sensex, Nifty close in green as easing Israel-Iran tensions lift sentimentThe BSE Sensex gained 394.50 points, or 0.54%, to close at 73,918.76. The NSE Nifty50 rose 119.10 points, or 0.52%, to settle at 23,242.10.

The inspection that hid more than it revealed, and the enforcement reform now neededIn the 10th part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines a case where detailed scrutiny of company records exposed inspection failures, missing disclosures and substantial under-reporting of coverable employees.

From Oman to Gujarat: The mega pipeline that could reshape India's energy futureEvery flare-up in the Strait of Hormuz sends shockwaves through global energy markets. Could a proposed 2,000-km undersea pipeline from Oman to Gujarat help India break free from that vulnerability? Let's find out.

Why the government cut Ujjwala LPG subsidy from nine to four cylinders a yearThe government has said the cut in subsidised Ujjwala LPG refills was driven by alleged diversion of cylinders for commercial use.

OpenAI files confidential IPO, eyes trillion-dollar valuation in AI boomThe company announced on Monday that it had submitted confidential paperwork for a stock market listing. However, OpenAI did not reveal the size of the offering, its expected valuation, or when it plans to go public.

Ujjwala LPG subsidy down to first 4 refills amid conflict-driven crude price surgeEarlier, PMUY beneficiaries received a Rs 300 subsidy on the first nine LPG refills annually. The government reduced this to four refills, citing average household consumption of about four cylinders per year.

India posts Q4 current account surplus as services exports surgeAccording to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Monday, the country's current account recorded a surplus of USD 7.1 billion, or 0.7 per cent of GDP, in the fourth quarter of 2025-26.

Oil, growth, inflation: CEA explains India's key risks amid West Asia crisisSpeaking to India Today, CEA V Anantha Nageswaran said India's economy remains strong, but uncertainty around the ongoing conflict and energy prices continues to pose risks.

Air India may introduce 'no meals' tickets on domestic flights to cut faresAir India is considering a no-meals ticket category for domestic passengers. The plan could begin on select short routes and lower fares for flyers who skip onboard food.

Builder delayed possession? Here's what homebuyers can doBuilder delayed possession of your flat? Learn your rights under RERA, when you can seek compensation or a refund, whether you must continue paying EMI, and what legal and financial options homebuyers have when projects are delayed.

Oil prices cannot stay this high for long, says Oil Minister Hardeep Singh PuriWhile fuel prices remain elevated for now, Puri's comments suggest that policymakers expect the pressure to ease over time.

Want to invest in SpaceX's IPO? Here's what retail investors need to knowAccess to the SpaceX IPO is not limited to the United States. The company has indicated that qualified investors in several countries may be able to buy shares, subject to local regulations and eligibility requirements.

Sensex, Nifty hit 2-month lows as Iran-Israel tensions push crude oil near $100Markets closed at nearly two-month lows after fresh Israeli strikes on Iran and Lebanon pushed crude prices sharply higher. The sell-off deepened across sectors as investors weighed inflation risks, global tech weakness and the path of interest rates.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

From AI trip planning to travel insurance: How are Indians redefining travel in 2026?Gone are the days when a holiday meant simply booking tickets and packing bags. Today's Indian traveller is more digital, more informed and more prepared, reshaping the travel landscape in the process.

India, US could finalise trade deal by July, says Commerce Minister Piyush GoyalThe Union Minister's remarks come at a time when India and the US are working to resolve outstanding issues in a proposed bilateral trade pact that is expected to improve market access and give Indian exporters an advantage over competitors in certain sectors.

AI stock sell-off hits Taiwan, South Korea after rally past IndiaAsian markets sank as investors dumped AI-linked chip stocks in Taiwan, South Korea and Japan. The sell-off has raised fresh questions over whether AI rally expectations had run ahead of earnings reality.

CIBIL score under 730? Getting a home or car loan could become tougher from 2027For many Indians, a home loan or car loan is often the biggest financial commitment they make. But from 2027, getting such loans could become more difficult if your CIBIL score is below 730, as banks prepare to follow stricter RBI guidelines on lending risk.

entertainment.headline: Singer Sapna Choudhary gets protection order, husband barred from contacting her Singer Sapna Choudhary has been granted interim protection by the Dwarka Mahila Court ahead of the premiere of her upcoming film Momacu. The court has restrained her husband from contacting or approaching her until the next hearing.

Elle trailer: The Legally Blonde prequel brings pink power to high school The first trailer for Elle introduces Elle Woods, played by Lexi Minetree, as a pink-loving teenager navigating high school in 1995. It sets up the friendships, romance and family bonds that shape the woman audiences later meet at Harvard.

Enola Holmes 3 trailer: Millie Bobby Brown races to save kidnapped Sherlock The trailer for Enola Holmes 3 promises the young detective's most dangerous case yet as she sets out to rescue her kidnapped brother, Sherlock Holmes. Starring Millie Bobby Brown and Henry Cavill, the Netflix film arrives on July 1, 2026.

It took every cell in my body to undo the bullying and prejudice: Kangana Ranaut Kangana Ranaut opened up about overcoming prejudice and finding strength through difficult phases of her life. The actor said bravery comes in many forms, from showing up during personal struggles to confronting criticism and self-doubt.

Actor Akansha Ranjan Kapoor, filmmaker Sharan Sharma set for a July wedding Actor Akansha Ranjan Kapoor and director Sharan Sharma are reportedly set to marry in Mumbai on July 11. The couple is planning a private celebration with close family and friends, followed by a reception on July 12.

Bigg Boss 20: Salman Khan to begin shoot in September, auditions to begin soon Salman Khan is set to begin filming Bigg Boss 20 on September 21 while juggling his film commitments. The move sets the stage for auditions, casting talks and fresh format details in the coming weeks.

Amid Peddi row, Kangana Ranaut says women actors can flag problematic scenes Kangana Ranaut has weighed in on the controversy surrounding Peddi, saying women actors should voice their concerns if a scene feels uncomfortable or problematic.

Jacob Elordi snaps back during a fan encounter in Japan: Don't touch me A video of actor Jacob Elordi confronting a fan who touched him during a photo request in Japan has gone viral on social media. The incident comes as buzz builds around his upcoming film The Dog Stars and dating rumours with model Kendall Jenner.

Katrina Kaif eyes OTT projects for her comeback: Sources Actor Katrina Kaif has reportedly resumed reading scripts and is considering OTT projects for her return to acting. The move could mark her digital debut, with the actor said to be looking for a role that aligns with her priorities after embracing motherhood.

Maa Behen's Dharna Durga tops IMDB popularity list, beats Madhuri and Triptii Dharna Durga finished ahead of Madhuri Dixit and Triptii Dimri on this week's IMDb Popular Indian Celebrities list. The ranking put the spotlight on Maa Behen as the Netflix film continued to draw attention for its humour and subversion of patriarchal tropes.

Edgy, neurotic, suicidal: Kangana Ranaut breaks down her early career image Actor Kangana Ranaut, in an exclusive interview, revisited the edgy and neurotic roles that shaped her early image. She said each career phase helped her resist typecasting and move towards new characters.

Teary-eyed Salman Khan pays last respects to friend Kumod Raney; Helen, Sohail join Salman Khan attended family friend Kumod Raney's funeral and was seen visibly emotional at the funeral. Salman was accompanied by mother Salma and brother Sohail Khan.

Girls' Generation's Sooyoung, Jung Kyung-ho split after 14 years; agency confirms Sooyoung and Jung Kyung-ho have ended their relationship after nearly 14 years together. The confirmed split follows online speculation after fans noticed changes in their social media follows.

At 94, director Singeetham Srinivasa Rao remains Indian cinema's great experimenter At 94, filmmaker Singeetham Srinivasa Rao is bringing Sing Geetham to screen after carrying the idea for nearly four decades. The project underlines his long-running habit of testing cinema's limits rather than following formula.

The men beside me during Peddi explain why Janhvi is hyper-sexualised in the film I watched Peddi alone, and the two men beside me kept laughing through Janhvi Kapoor's scenes while invading my space. If this is how some people react in a theatre, maybe it's time we questioned what we're normalising on screen.

Arjun Rampal on broken marriage, mother's cancer and the years that tested him Actor Arjun Rampal opened up about the period when his marriage to model Mehr Jesia was ending, and his mother had cancer. He said his partner, fashion designer Gabriella Demetriades, became an anchor as grief, loss and introspection reshaped his life.

Baby Do Die Do teaser: Huma Qureshi is a deaf-mute hitwoman in mystery-thriller Huma Qureshi and Nachiket Samant have reunited for Baby Do Die Do, whose teaser introduces her as a desi hitwoman. The crime thriller also opens a new chapter for Huma as she makes her debut as a producer with Saqib Saleem.

Governors Ball 2026: Jennie, Stray Kids and A$AP Rocky wow fans with their act Governors Ball 2026 ended in New York after a weekend led by Lorde, Stray Kids, Jennie, KATSEYE and A$AP Rocky. The line-up underscored how global acts and K-pop are reshaping major festival stages.

Ravi Mohan-Aarti divorce: Court rejects plea for more time in maintenance case The Madras High Court dismissed actor Ravi Mohan's plea seeking more time in the interim maintenance case filed by estranged wife Aarti Ravi. The order keeps the Family Welfare Court bound to decide her pending plea within two weeks.

general news.headline: US must respond: Trump says Iran shot down Apache helicopter in HormuzTrump claimed Iran shot down the helicopter and vowed a response, while the US military said the cause of the crash near Oman remains under investigation after both crew members were rescued by a Navy drone.

Congress's Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination cancelled, party protestsCongress Rajya Sabha nominee Meenakshi Natarajan's candidature from Madhya Pradesh was rejected after election authorities accepted objections alleging she failed to disclose details of a Telangana court matter in her nomination affidavit.

Indian-born CEO faces loss of US citizenship over H-1B visa fraudNeeraj Sharma, an India-born CEO convicted of H-1B visa fraud, is among 17 naturalised Americans who are set to lose their citizenship as the Trump administration launches the largest denaturalisation drive in recent US history.

Nasa reveals Artemis-III astronauts, selects diverse crew from US, EuropeNasa has unveiled the four astronauts selected for Artemis III, a mission that will conduct critical docking demonstrations and collect scientific data to help pave the way for future lunar landings.

6 satellites in 16 months: Pakistan has quietly built a surveillance networkPakistan has launched six Earth-observation satellites in 16 months with substantial Chinese support. The growing constellation is seen as strengthening Islamabad's ability to monitor Indian territory and strategic activity.

Indian couple, 9-year-old son jump to their deaths from London luxury apartmentRakesh Pai (47) and Aditi Paralkar (46) and their nine-year-old son, Sid, fell from their apartment in the UNCLE tower block in London. According to the Daily Mail, the couple allegedly took the step after doctors reportedly said there were no further treatment options for their terminally ill son.

Test pilots, seasoned astronauts, surgeon: Meet Nasa's Artemis-III crewNasa has named Randy Bresnik, Luca Parmitano, Frank Rubio and Andre Douglas for Artemis III. Their mission will carry out key docking tests and gather data for future lunar expeditions.

India deployed nuclear warheads for the first time: Sipri reportIndia deployed nuclear warheads for the first time, according to the annual report of Sipri, a global watchdog on arms control. Data reveals that India deployed 12 nuclear warheads in 2025, and China increased the number of deployed warheads from 24 to 34.

Rs 2.5 lakh Japan school trip costs more than 25 years of Kendriya Vidyalaya feesA viral social media post about a Class 8 student's Rs 2.5 lakh school trip to Japan has revived a familiar debate: when educational experiences start costing more than education itself. The amount is so large that it could cover the annual fees of a Kendriya Vidyalaya student for more than two decades.

What Albert Einstein's viral marksheet teaches us about marks and successA resurfaced image of Albert Einstein's marksheet has renewed discussion about how student ability is judged. The attention on his uneven grades underscores that exam scores may not capture a person's full potential.

England captain Ben Stokes could retire after nightclub controversyBen Stokes' England future has been thrown into doubt after a nightclub controversy triggered an ECB investigation. Reports suggest the Test captain could lose the captaincy, take a break from cricket, or even retire from international cricket altogether.

India freezes Starlink's approval after concerns over use during Iran warStarlink's India entry has reportedly hit a roadblock, with security agencies withholding final clearance over concerns linked to its global operations and the reported use of its terminals during the Iran conflict.

The men beside me during Peddi explain why Janhvi is hyper-sexualised in the filmI watched Peddi alone, and the two men beside me kept laughing through Janhvi Kapoor's scenes while invading my space. If this is how some people react in a theatre, maybe it's time we questioned what we're normalising on screen.

How come Mamata was unaware of corruption in Trinamool? Rebel MP as crisis deepensThe three-term MP from the Birbhum Lok Sabha constituency said that the massive corruption scandals have completely overshadowed and neutralised all the developmental work achieved by the TMC government in the state.

19 children, 7 pregnant women killed: Inside details of bloodbath in POKAs per reports emerging from Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir, dozens have been killed after Pakistani forces launched a violent crackdown against protesters belonging to the Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC) movement, which has been protesting against alleged human rights violations, inflation, unemployment and economic distress.

Highlights: Mamata Banerjee should be arrested, says Bengal minister after CID visitWest Bengal TMC Crisis Highlights: The Trinamool Congress' deepening internal crisis spilled into the open on Tuesday as the party leadership and a rebel faction traded sharp attacks amid claims that a group of MPs is preparing to back the BJP-led NDA in Parliament. At a press conference, TMC Lok Sabha Chief Whip Kalyan Banerjee dismissed the dissidents as "gaddars" and "traitors", daring them to resign from the party and seek a fresh mandate from voters. Banerjee claimed the rebels stood "exposed" the moment they met senior BJP leader Bhupender Yadav and accused them of abandoning party workers while enjoying the privileges of office. He also took aim at rebel MP Sharmila Sarkar, alleging she had remained silent on issues such as the RG Kar hospital controversy. The remarks came hours after rebel leader and Lok Sabha MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar claimed that nearly 20 Trinamool MPs had decided to write to Speaker Om Birla expressing support for the NDA. The dissident camp is expected to argue that Ghosh Dastidar remains the party's chief whip in the Lok Sabha, setting the stage for a high-stakes battle over the Trinamool's parliamentary leadership and future. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the live updates.

Watch: UP Police exam sparks travel chaos, massive crowds at train stationsCrowded platforms, overnight waits and packed trains told one story after the UP Police exam. Beneath the chaos lay a deeper concern shared by thousands of candidates hoping their years of preparation are not wasted again.

Govt clears new Rs 13,000-crore Great Nicobar airport, drops INS Baaz expansionThe new airport will be built at Chingen near Galathea Bay and is expected to serve both civilian and military aviation requirements. Defence sources said the decision followed studies that found extending INS Baaz's existing 4,500-foot runway to around 10,000 feet was impractical because of topography and infra-related constraints.

8 killed after blast triggers massive fire at illegal cracker godown in JaipurOfficials said preliminary findings suggest highly inflammable material stored inside the premises may have triggered the explosion, though the exact cause is under investigation.

Jacqueline Fernandez moves SC against charges in Rs 200-crore extortion caseED alleged Jacqueline Fernandez was in constant touch with conman Sukesh Chandrasekhar despite being aware of his criminal background and received expensive gifts from him through intermediary Pinky Irani.

From Oman to Gujarat: The mega pipeline that could reshape India's energy futureEvery flare-up in the Strait of Hormuz sends shockwaves through global energy markets. Could a proposed 2,000-km undersea pipeline from Oman to Gujarat help India break free from that vulnerability? Let's find out.

CM Joseph Vijay's first month: Promises, punctuality, and a few stumblesVijay's first month as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister has been bold, ambitious, and watched very closely. The opening chapter is more substance than spectacle, but not without blemish.

Hope Pak will be held accountable: India condemns massacre of Kashmiris in POKThe remarks come days after a crackdown by Pakistani authorities in Rawalakot reportedly left at least 27 people dead. While official figures have put the death toll at 27, videos circulating on social media have claimed that the number of casualties may be significantly higher.

1 lakh customers bought this SUV: Offers 28kmpl efficiency, price starts at Rs 10.50 lakhMaruti Suzuki's latest SUV has crossed the 1 lakh sales milestone in just eight months. Priced from Rs 10.50 lakh and offering up to 28.65kmpl efficiency, the SUV has quickly become a strong seller.

sports.headline: England captain Ben Stokes could retire after nightclub controversy Ben Stokes' England future has been thrown into doubt after a nightclub controversy triggered an ECB investigation. Reports suggest the Test captain could lose the captaincy, take a break from cricket, or even retire from international cricket altogether.

From Alana King to Nensi Patel: Indian-origin stars to watch out for India head into the Women's T20 World Cup 2026 as one of the title contenders after their ODI World Cup triumph. But beyond Harmanpreet Kaur's side, several Indian-origin players representing other nations are also set to leave their mark.

A World Cup of chaos: Ex-England star blasts hosts USA over visa mess Ian Wright has labelled the FIFA World Cup 2026 the "World Cup of chaos" amid mounting concerns over visa denials and travel disruptions. The former England star's criticism follows the exclusion of African referee Omar Artan from the tournament.

Pakistan endure Women's T20 World Cup warm-up horror after shock defeat to Scotland Pakistan's preparations for the Women's T20 World Cup suffered another setback as they slumped to a defeat against Scotland. Pakistan's batting faltered, leaving Fatima Sana's side with plenty to address ahead of a daunting group-stage campaign.

Gaikwad proves selectors wrong as India-A win thriller in Dambulla India A beat Sri Lanka A in an absolute thriller in Dambulla on Tuesday, June 9. Playing their first match in the tri-series, India dragged the match back in the final 3 overs to win by 8 runs.

Indian football crisis nears its end as AIFF and ISL teams agree on club-led model The Indian Super League is set to move forward after the AIFF agreed in principle to a two-year club-led model. The breakthrough, backed by the Sports Ministry, clears the path for the 2026-27 season to begin on September 4.

Group B preview: Can West Indies, Sri Lanka surprise the favourites? Women's T20 World Cup: England and New Zealand are widely considered favourites to reach the semifinals, but West Indies and Sri Lanka have the ability to spring surprises. Meanwhile, Ireland and Scotland will be looking to cause a few upsets along the way.

Arjun Tendulkar shines in Mumbai T20 League, gets big dressing room ovation Arjun Tendulkar produced a stunning all-round display to power Arcs Andheri to a dominant win over Bandra Blasters in the T20 Mumbai League. The youngster was later rewarded with an emotional dressing-room ovation led by captain Shivam Dube.

Workhorse Mohammed Siraj withdrawn from India's T20I tour of England and Ireland Mohammed Siraj has been rested from India's T20Is in Ireland and England under a workload plan. Prasidh Krishna replaces him as India manage resources before a long white-ball season, with Siraj retained for the ODI leg.

FIFA denies tickets to Iran football fans just days ahead of World Cup Iran's football federation has accused FIFA of pulling its World Cup ticket allocation just days before the tournament begins, leaving thousands of supporters in limbo after many had already booked flights, accommodation and travel plans to follow the national team.

Rohit Sharma and Hardik Pandya cleared fit for ODIs vs Afghanistan IND vs AFG: Hardik Pandya and Rohit Sharma have been cleared for India's ODI series against Afghanistan. Their return strengthens the batting-bowling balance, while Virat Kohli remains currently sidelined with a hamstring injury.

Footballer detained, referee deported: FIFA World Cup is a mess before kick-offJust days before the beginning of football's greatest spectacle, the FIFA World Cup has run into a myriad of controversies. The latest of those involves Africa's best football referee.

Bangladesh T20 captain reacts to WC boycott: Can play in Pak, then why not India?Litton Das has challenged Bangladesh's official line on pulling out of the T20 World Cup in India over security. His account raises fresh questions about why the call was eventually made.

SL A vs IND A highlights: India beat Sri Lanka by 8 runs in Dambulla thrillerSri Lanka A vs India A highlights: Sri Lanka-A saw their final three wickets go down in the 49th over, as the Ruturaj Gaikwad-led India snatched away an 8-run win over the hosts in some formidable clutch style. 2 wickets and a run out in Arshad Khan's penultimate over marked the end of a thrilling clash in Dambulla, which did not even have a Vaibhav Sooryavanshi show.

No rivals, beyond medals: How yogasana became more than a hunt for gloryAt the first World Yogasana Championship, athletes and coaches from 78 countries competed while often helping one another on and off the mat. The event suggested yogasana's strongest claim is not just medals, but a transnational culture of family, self-improvement and shared purpose.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is back: When and where to watch India A vs Sri Lanka AVaibhav Sooryavanshi returns as India A open the Dambulla tri-series against Sri Lanka A on Tuesday. Tilak Varma will lead a young Indian side in the tri-series, which will also feature Afghanistan A side.

What really happened at London nightclub Ben Stokes and England teammate visitedBen Stokes and Gus Atkinson are being investigated after a late-night altercation in London following England's win over New Zealand. The episode has delayed England's second-Test selection and reopened uneasy questions over leadership, discipline and control.

No Virat Kohli. Will Rohit Sharma play ODIs vs Afghanistan?India are awaiting medical clearance on Rohit Sharma for the Afghanistan ODI series. Hardik Pandya has been cleared, while Virat Kohli has been ruled out with a hamstring tear.

You're a gift to the sport: Andre Agassi hails Chwalinska's Roland Garros runAndre Agassi delivered an emotional message to Maja Chwalinska after her dream run to the Roland Garros final came to an end. The American legend hailed the Polish qualifier's historic achievement and called her "a gift to the sport."

Ben Stokes in trouble? England cricket probe 'nightclub incident' after Lord's TestEngland captain Ben Stokes and pacer Gus Atkinson are under investigation after an alleged breach of team protocols following the first Test against New Zealand. The incident has delayed squad decisions for the second Test and revived scrutiny of England's off-field culture.

Mandaviya backs club-led model to secure ISL futureUnion Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya has asked the AIFF and ISL clubs to form a joint task force to settle their financial dispute. The move is aimed at securing a stable business model before the 2026-27 season begins.

Africa's best referee denied entry to USA, set to be ruled out of FIFA World CupAfrica's top referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan has reportedly been denied entry into the United States and sent back to Turkey ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident has intensified concerns over visa and travel issues affecting tournament personnel.

They said you'd never win: Djokovic writes moving tribute to champion ZverevFollowing Alexander Zverev's historic French Open breakthrough, Novak Djokovic wrote a deeply personal tribute to his long-time friend, lauding the German's resilience in silencing critics who claimed he would never win a Grand Slam.

Shreyanka takes 4 as India spin to win against West Indies in T20 World Cup warm-upWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Bharti Fulmali's unbeaten fifty and Shreyanka Patil's four wickets gave India a 26-run warm-up win over West Indies in Cardiff. The outing offered useful game time to India's bench before matches against England and Pakistan.

Suryakumar Yadav answers T20I snub with Mumbai T20 League blitzDropped from India's T20I squads for Ireland and England, Suryakumar Yadav struck an unbeaten 72 in the Mumbai T20 League at Wankhede. The innings revived his run of form and renewed focus on his place in India's plans.

India's delicate plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Help his game, not ruin itIndia's batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will be eased into the national set-up after his maiden T20I call-up. The team wants to protect the 15-year-old's natural game rather than overload him with advice.

Test team in transition? Sunil Gavaskar disagrees with coach Gautam Gambhir's takeAs India enter a crucial World Test Championship phase, batting legend Sunil Gavaskar has urged the young, evolving batting unit to step up and focus on long-form discipline rather than relying on the transition narrative.

Christian Eriksen in good spirits, set for discharge after mid-match heart scareChristian Eriksen is expected to be discharged from hospital soon after making a positive recovery from a mid-match collapse during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine. The Danish midfielder quickly regained consciousness, with doctors confirming he remains in stable condition.

Yogasana Super League: An IPL-themed competition to boost yoga's Olympic dreamYogasana Bharat says the Yogasana Super League is nearly ready for launch this year. The league sits at the centre of a broader push for Asian Games, Commonwealth Games and Olympic recognition.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rise is dangerous for cricket: Greg ChappellFormer Australia cricketer Greg Chappell has warned that T20 cricket is approaching a tipping point, arguing that the format has become heavily skewed in favour of batters. Concerned about the growing imbalance between bat and ball, Chappell proposed three rule changes that he believes could restore balance to the game.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
